Imagine being three years old and knowing you are different from everyone around you. Mei stood in her new preschool and looked at all the other children. They were all white. They all spoke English. Mei had dark hair and brown eyes. She spoke Cantonese with her grandfather. When her grandfather tried to leave that first morning, Mei screamed. She threw a terrible fit. Because she knew if he left, she would be completely alone. Her grandfather stayed. Every day for two years, he stayed at preschool with her. This is Mei's story. It is about growing up between two worlds. About feeling shame for the people she loved most. About learning that she did not have to choose. About discovering that being caught between two cultures could create something beautiful. Something new. A bridge.
